root_cause.text: The remote API exposes upstream and downstream project information through the `@Exported` annotation on the `getDownstreamProjects` and `getUpstreamProjects` methods in `AbstractProject` without verifying if the user has `Item.READ` permission for those linked projects.
root_cause.evidence: The patch removes the `@Exported` annotation from `getDownstreamProjects()` and `getUpstreamProjects()`, and introduces new methods (`getDownstreamProjectsForApi` and `getUpstreamProjectsForApi`) that explicitly check `p.hasPermission(Item.READ)` before including a project in the exported API results.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The remote API exposes upstream and downstream project information through the `@Exported` annotation on the `getDownstreamProjects` and `getUpstreamProjects` methods in `AbstractProject` without verifying if the user has `Item.READ` permission for those linked projects.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch removes the `@Exported` annotation from `getDownstreamProjects()` and `getUpstreamProjects()`, and introduces new methods (`getDownstreamProjectsForApi` and `getUpstreamProjectsForApi`) that explicitly check `p.hasPermission(Item.READ)` before including a project in the exported API results.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a permission check to prevent unauthorized disclosure of build details in kept-because messages.
intent_record.what: Added an Item.READ permission check using a ternary operator before passing the build information to the message formatter.
intent_record.how: Prevents unauthorized users from viewing specific build details, substituting a question mark if the user lacks read permissions.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add read permission checks to exported upstream and downstream project APIs to prevent unauthorized disclosure.
intent_record.what: Removed the @Exported annotation from existing project dependency getters and created new API-specific getters that filter the lists.
intent_record.how: Prevents unauthorized disclosure of project dependencies by enforcing Item.READ permission checks before returning upstream and downstream projects through the API.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add test to verify authorization checks for upstream and downstream projects in the API.
intent_record.what: Added a new test class AbstractProject2Test with a test method upstreamDownstreamExportApi.
intent_record.how: The test verifies that upstream and downstream projects are only exposed in the API if the requesting user has the appropriate read permissions for those projects.

grouping_result.intent: Add a permission check to prevent unauthorized disclosure of build details in kept-because messages.
Add read permission checks to exported upstream and downstream project APIs to prevent unauthorized disclosure.
Add test to verify authorization checks for upstream and downstream projects in the API.

relevance_review.reason: The group addresses permission checks for kept-because messages, which is unrelated to the vulnerability involving upstream and downstream project exposure in the remote API.
The group directly addresses the vulnerability by adding the missing read permission checks to the upstream and downstream project APIs, which was the root cause of the unauthorized disclosure.
The group adds tests specifically to verify the authorization checks for upstream and downstream projects, directly testing the fix for the vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This statement returns the downstream projects directly without filtering based on Item.READ permissions.
This statement returns the upstream projects directly without filtering based on Item.READ permissions.
The Exported annotation exposes the downstream projects via the API, which combined with the lack of permission checks leads to unauthorized disclosure.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement directly returns downstream projects without checking if the user has Item.READ permission, leading to information disclosure through the remote API.
This statement directly returns upstream projects without checking if the user has Item.READ permission, leading to information disclosure through the remote API.

diff --git a/core/src/main/java/hudson/model/AbstractProject.java b/core/src/main/java/hudson/model/AbstractProject.java
index cf01c0e9ea..59d13cdbdc 100644
--- a/core/src/main/java/hudson/model/AbstractProject.java
+++ b/core/
bic_trace_step.reason: The methods `getDownstreamProjects()` and `getUpstreamProjects()` return the list of downstream and upstream projects directly using `Jenkins.getInstance().getDependencyGraph()`. There are no permission checks or filters applied to the returned lists to verify if the user has `Item.READ` permission for the linked projects, which matches the vulnerability description.
